questions.question: Where was the 5th edition of Lok Samvardhan Parv organized?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Minority Affairs, Government of India, is organising the 5th edition of Lok Samvardhan Parv at Marine Drive Ground, Shanmugham Road, Kochi, Kerala, from 26th August to 4th September 2025. Lok Samvardhan Parv is a flagship initiative to provide artisans, craftspersons, weavers, culinary experts, and entrepreneurs from minority communities with market linkages and national exposure. The Kochi edition is the first-ever in Kerala, reflecting the city’s cosmopolitan spirit and diversity.

questions.question: Vibrant Villages Programme is implemented by which ministry?
questions.explanation: Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation inaugurated a two-day workshop on Vibrant Villages Programme (VVP) in New Delhi. Vibrant Villages Programme (VVP) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme running from 2022-23 to 2025-26. It targets 2,967 villages in 46 blocks across 19 districts along India’s northern border in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Sikkim, Uttarakhand, and Union Territory of Ladakh. It aims to stop outmigration, strengthen border security, and encourage people to stay in native villages. Vibrant Villages Programme is implemented by the Union Ministry of Home Affairs.

questions.question: What is the name of the biggest financial inclusion initiative in the world launched by India?
questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) completes 11 years on August 28, 2025, as the world’s largest financial inclusion programme. It was launched on August 28, 2014. Its objective is to promote financial inclusion via banking, savings, credit, insurance, pensions, and remittance services. PMJDY supports Direct Benefit Transfers (DBTs) and forms the backbone of government economic initiatives. Before PMJDY, 59% of households and 35% of adults in India had bank accounts. After 11 years, almost 100% households and over 90% adults have bank accounts. 56.2 crore accounts opened, including 37.5 crore rural/semi-urban and 18.7 crore urban accounts. 56% accounts held by women, promoting gender inclusion.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as Vice President and Chief Risk Officer of the New Development Bank for a term of five years?
questions.explanation: New Development Bank (NDB), established by BRICS nations—Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa—appointed Dr. Rajiv Ranjan as Vice President and Chief Risk Officer for a five-year term. Ranjan started his career at Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in 1989 and was Executive Director and Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) member since May 2022. NDB finances India’s infrastructure projects in transport, clean energy, water, sanitation, environmental protection, and digital infrastructure. India holds the largest NDB project portfolio in BRICS at $7.5 billion, second only to China.

questions.question: Matsya Shakti Project is associated with which sector?
questions.explanation: The Matsya Shakti Project was launched on August 28, 2025, by Shri George Kurian, Union Minister of State for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry, Dairying and Minority Affairs, at Kovalam. It is implemented by the Vizhinjam Regional Centre of ICAR–Central Marine Fisheries Research Institute (CMFRI) under the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare. A Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed between the Ministry of Minority Affairs and CMFRI for programme rollout. It is conceived under the Pradhan Mantri Virasat Ka Samvardhan (PM VIKAS) scheme. The project will enhance the socio-economic well-being of fisherfolk families through a year-long phased training programme.

questions.question: Samvatsari Day is a traditional festival celebrated in which religion?
questions.explanation: Samvatsari was recently celebrated as the most important day of Paryushan Parva, a holy observance in Jainism. The festival embodies the spirit of forgiveness, compassion, atonement, and self-purification. On Samvatsari, Jains seek forgiveness from family, friends, and strangers for any harm caused knowingly or unknowingly. It is a day of reunion, humility, and reflection on personal actions. The occasion reinforces the Jain principles of ahimsa (non-violence) and spiritual cleansing.

questions.question: Which state is the host of 41st Indian Association of Tour Operators (IATO) annual conference?
questions.explanation: Special Chief Secretary, Department of Tourism, Ajay Jain announced that the 41st Indian Association of Tour Operators (IATO) Convention will be hosted by Andhra Pradesh in Visakhapatnam in 2026. The announcement was made during the 40th IATO annual convention held in Puri, Odisha from August 22 to 24, 2025. The three-day event followed the theme ‘Rejuvenate Inbound@2030’. Andhra Pradesh government has granted industry status to the tourism sector. Focus is on world-class infrastructure, including new airports, to boost tourism. All participants were invited to attend the 41st IATO Convention in Visakhapatnam in 2026.

questions.question: In which state was the joint Army-ITBP drill “Achook Prahar” held in 2025?
questions.explanation: The Indian Army’s Spear Corps and the Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP) conducted Exercise Achook Prahar in central Arunachal Pradesh (Aug 25–28, 2025) to validate joint operational preparedness. The drill involved mortars, machine guns, rockets, and grenades, highlighting interoperability in high-altitude warfare. The ITBP, raised in 1962 after the Sino-Indian War, guards the 3,488 km India-China border through 197 Border Outposts at altitudes up to 18,750 ft. It functions under the Ministry of Home Affairs, is a specialised mountain force, and has the motto “Shaurya–Dridhata–Karma Nishtha”.

questions.question: Recently, India signed a pact with Japan on which carbon-credit mechanism?
questions.explanation: India and Japan recently signed a Memorandum of Cooperation in Aug 2025 on the Joint Crediting Mechanism (JCM), a Japanese initiative that allows investment in low-carbon technologies in developing countries. The resulting emission reductions are credited to Japan’s carbon account, helping it meet climate targets. For India, the JCM will bring investment, technology transfer, and capacity building, while also enabling carbon credit trading under Article 6.2 of the Paris Agreement. This supports India’s NDC goals: reducing emission intensity by 45% by 2030, achieving 50% non-fossil fuel power capacity, and creating a carbon sink of 2.5–3 billion tonnes CO₂ through afforestation.

questions.question: Recently (July 2025), the Uttarakhand forest department approved diversion of forest land in the Bhagirathi eco-sensitive zone for which project? a) Rishikesh–Karnaprayag Railway Line b) Netala bypass under the Char Dham road project c) Tehri Hydro Power Project Expansion d) All-weather Kedarnath Ropeway

options.label: B
options.text: Netala bypass under the Char Dham road project

answer.label: B
answer.text: Netala bypass under the Char Dham road project

questions.explanation: The Uttarakhand forest department granted in-principle approval for the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) to use 17.5 hectares of forest land to construct the Netala bypass (8.07 km) along NH-34, part of the Char Dham road project. The stretch falls within the fragile Bhagirathi eco-sensitive zone, where the Supreme Court and High Power Committee (HPC) had earlier capped road width at 5.5 metres and advised against large-scale tree felling. Environmentalists allege the clearance violates these directives, with the risk of destabilising slopes and worsening landslides and monsoon disasters in the region.
